item.title: ExxonMobil awards completions contract for work offshore Nigeria
item.description: ExxonMobil picks Weatherford for deepwater efforts in African country

item.title: McDermott to share refinancing plan update in 'near term'
item.description: The contractor's accrued liabilities during the first quarter of 2026 stood at $1.824 billion

item.title: Petrobras and Saipem sign deal for decommissioning offshore Brazil
item.description: One-year agreement to define framework for technical and operational collaboration between companies

item.title: Petrobras revives Albacora production with ‘vintage FPSO’
item.description: Brazilian oil company also tendering for new FPSO for Campos basin field

item.title: Iran’s new Persian Gulf Authority draws lines of control around Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tehran claims holding sway over a wide area from its own shores to UAE ports of Fujairah and Umm Al Quwain

item.title: EU has got methane rules wrong, says Equinor
item.description: Equinor exports around 120 billion cubic metres per year of gas to Europe

item.title: UK to curb judicial reviews that stall giant wind farm projects
item.description: Government set to restrict use of key legal weapon used against major renewable energy and grid projects

item.title: Equinor and Aker BP swap assets to push ahead major Norway projects
item.description: Deal between Equinor and Aker BP includes Ringvei Vest and Wisting developments

item.title: Contractor wins coveted deal for multibillion-dollar Qatar megaproject
item.description: Bul Hanine project is crucial to Qatar’s strategy of maintaining and expanding the production profile of nation’s largest offshore oilfield

item.title: Wood wins coveted deal for multibillion-dollar Qatar megaproject
item.description: Bul Hanine project is crucial to Qatar’s strategy of maintaining and expanding the production profile of nation’s largest offshore oilfield

item.title: Adnoc offshore unit chief bullish on US prospects despite potential headwinds
item.description: ZMI chief executive Ali El Ali: ‘Business as usual’ amid Middle East war

item.title: Ithaca Energy making big strides on new UK projects
item.description: Company focused on Fotla in the North Sea and Tornado in the Shetlands, plus Rosebank and Cambo

item.title: BP wins big in Indonesia's latest acreage awards
item.description: Blocks also awarded to Inpex, Mubadala Energy, Medco Energi, Bumi Armada, Proteknik Gagah Energi and Sele Energi

item.title: Inpex advances Indonesia gas megaproject
item.description: Abadi will produce 9.5 million tonnes per annum of LNG, plus gas for domestic market

item.title: Decision phase nears for deals on $10 billion-plus Middle East project
item.description: Dorra is being developed by a joint venture owned by Aramco and Kuwait Petroleum Corporation (KPC) and is expected to produce 1 billion cubic feet per day of gas and 84,000 barrels per day of condensate

item.title: Worley clinches contract for Australia gas infrastructure projects
item.description: Contractor will support APA Group in gas transmission and storage projects

item.title: Worley wins contract for Australia gas infrastructure projects
item.description: Contractor will support APA Group in gas transmission and storage projects

item.title: US shale player’s $2.6 billion Permian bet spurs $4 billion auction haul for government coffers
item.description: Devon Energy laps other contestants in lease sale for New Mexico and Texas tracts

item.title: Crude prices fall on signs of tankers clearing Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Reports of some vessels making it through the waterway sent WTI tumbling to below $100 per barrel

item.title: Petrobras and Pemex to meet again on potential partnership
item.description: Companies discussing investment opportunities in Gulf of Mexico

item.title: Iraq switches floating power plant to gas as Middle East conflict hits revenues
item.description: Orhan Ali Khan, capable of supplying up to 4% of Iraq's electricity needs, will be relocated to another port

item.title: Second FPSO eyed for Falkland Islands project
item.description: Navitas Petroleum inks tentative deal for another production vessel at Sea Lion as work on initial FPSO is moved out of the Middle East due to war

item.title: Decades-long battle over drilling in Alaska refuge set to heat up again
item.description: Trump administration eager to drill in Arctic National Wildlife Refuge as green groups promise a fight ahead

item.title: Petrobras eyes two FPSOs to produce from ‘gigantic’ pre-salt area
item.description: Brazilian oil company studying opportunities for Aram block in Santos basin

item.title: BW Energy sanctions $860 million projects in Gabon and Brazil
item.description: Company takes final investment decisions on Bourdon scheme in West Africa and $450-million Golfinho infill drilling campaign in South America

item.title: Gasunie calls for EU-wide strategic gas reserves
item.description: European gas storage is in the spotlight as refilling season gets underway

item.title: UK climate adviser calls for clarity on UK gas grid
item.description: UK energy infrastructure must be adapted to mitigate against climate change risks

item.title: QatarEnergy buys into Shell’s stake in three exploration blocks offshore Uruguay
item.description: Qatari state player has picked up stakes in blocks OFF-2, OFF-4, and OFF-7 in Uruguay.

item.title: UK player sells stake in South Africa exploration asset
item.description: Eco Atlantic and Israeli player Navitas enter definitive farm-down agreement for South Africa block

item.title: UK player sells stake in Oraneg basin exploration asset
item.description: Eco Atlantic and Israeli player Navitas enter definitive farm-down agreement for South Africa block
